steps.title: Find the median of data set A
steps.content:
Data set A contains 21 values, so the median is at position $\frac{21+1}{2}=11$ when ordered from smallest to largest.

Build a cumulative count from the frequency table:
  • 0 eagles: 1 day (cumulative: 1)
  • 1 eagle: 3 days (cumulative: 4)
  • 2 eagles: 4 days (cumulative: 8)
  • 3 eagles: 5 days (cumulative: 13)
Position 11 falls in the range [9, 13], which corresponds to 3 eagles. Therefore, the median of data set A is 3.

steps.title: Find the median of data set B and compare
steps.content: Data set B has 20 values after 19 is removed, so the median is the average of positions 10 and 11. Both positions still fall in the 3-eagle group, so median B is $\frac{3+3}{2}=3$. Desmos can verify both medians from expanded lists. Both medians equal 3, matching choice B.
